text: know is a Rust CLI tool for local semantic search over project knowledge bases. It uses clap for argument parsing with two subcommands: build (generate embeddings) and search (find best match).
The project uses fastembed 4.x with the paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2 model (384 dimensions). The model is auto-downloaded on first use and cached under ~/.cache/know/models/.
Embeddings are serialized with bincode for fast I/O. Each EmbeddingEntry stores id, text, and vector (Vec<f32>). knowledge.json is plain JSON with an array of {id, text} objects.

Search uses cosine similarity with a configurable threshold (default 0.45). Results are filtered by threshold, sorted descending by score, and truncated to top-k (default 1). The --top (-n) flag controls how many results are returned.

The build command reads knowledge.json, generates embeddings for all entries using fastembed, and writes them to embeddings.bin. Custom input/output paths can be specified with -k/--knowledge and -o/--output.

The project was designed to keep AI agent memory clean by offloading project-specific facts to a local file. knowledge.json can be committed to git; embeddings.bin should be gitignored (it's large and regenerable).

Search results are printed as [KNOW [id]]: text (score: 0.xxx). If no id is set, the format is [KNOW]: text (score: 0.xxx). When no results pass the threshold, it prints [KNOW]: No relevant information found (below 0.xx threshold).
The project has four modules: main.rs (CLI + dispatch), embed.rs (fastembed model wrapping), search.rs (cosine similarity + top-k ranking), storage.rs (JSON + bincode I/O).
Both embed_texts and embed_query create a fresh TextEmbedding model instance each call. The model cache directory is set to ~/.cache/know/models/ via dirs::cache_dir().
Error handling uses anyhow with context messages. File I/O errors, format errors, and embedding failures all return Results with descriptive error messages.
